mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack triggers aggressive monetary accommodation and recession fears, causing equity valuations to contract over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving immediate positive equity momentum over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient corporate output that outpaces rate hike headwinds, generating short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration predicts negative or flat 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 25 basis points monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 5-year sample, or the signal consistently underperforms during periods of productivity stagnation.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% monthly forces central banks to prioritize price stability over growth, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as real discount rates rise.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 20-day returns in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during supply-driven inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures reveals underlying demand fragility, causing negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shocks in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production combined with flattening payroll growth signal an imminent supply-side contraction, triggering short-term negative equity momentum as recession probabilities spike.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of strong export demand.
